features.name: Nimble Escape
features.text: The goblin can take the Disengage or Hide action as a bonus action on each of its turns.

features.name: Grimeback Hexer
features.text: Uses the Grimeback Hexer stat block. Gristla has all the same features and actions as a Grimeback Hexer, including Nimble Escape, Multiattack, Dagger, Sludge Bolt, and Spellcasting.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.text: The hexer makes two Dagger attacks or two Sludge Bolt attacks. It can replace one attack with Spellcasting.

actions.name: Spellcasting
actions.text:
The hexer casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12):

At will: mage hand, minor illusion

2/day each: thunderwave, shield

summary.description: Mogrot's daughter, a Grimeback hexer in her own right. She lives in a surprisingly well-appointed room on the upper level of the Dripfort, with an actual blanket, a cracked mirror, and a decorative collection of colorful surface-world bottles. She is engaged in a secret relationship with Skivvy, the scout captain. After midnight, Skivvy sneaks up to her room to read her terrible love poetry. If their relationship were discovered, Mogrot would be furious.

encounters.description: Found in her room on the upper level (Location 11b). After midnight, Skivvy is with her.

groups.creatures: Gristla and Skivvy together after midnight. If noise is made, 2 Grimeback thug guards from the platform arrive at end of round 1.
groups.treasure: 234 sp, a painted porcelain figurine (30 gp), a black pearl necklace (100 gp), a brass hand mirror (5 gp), an ornamental bronze helmet (10 gp)

behavior.text: Spending time in her room on the upper level, tending her collection of surface-world bottles.
After midnight, lying on her bed listening to Skivvy read terrible poetry with an expression that might be adoration or pity.
